summary: Dependency-confusion shim exfiltrates environment variables via preinstall hook
details: @doaction/wasm-loader@99.99.99 exhibits the canonical dependency-confusion attack shape: a scoped package name published at an artificially high version (99.99.99) designed to win npm's version resolution over an internal package of the same name. The package.json declares a preinstall lifecycle hook (`"preinstall": "node scripts/postinstall.js"`) that require()s `@doaction/shared/bin/postinstall.js`, and src/index.js re-exports `collectEnv`, `sendToDatadog`, and `reportEnvToDatadog` from the `@doaction/shared` dependency. The advertised purpose is to collect installer environment variables and send them to Datadog. On any `npm install` of a project that mistakenly resolves to this public shim instead of an internal package of the same name, the preinstall hook executes the @doaction/shared payload without user consent, harvesting environment variables (which routinely include CI/CD secrets, API tokens, cloud credentials) and shipping them to a third-party endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
